pattern: Since a good subsequence only ever needs its next element to be exactly one away from its last element, tracking how many good subsequences currently end in each value -- and the total sum of their elements -- lets every new number extend the ones ending in its neighboring values in a single pass.
hints: Keep a running count, for every value that has appeared so far, of how many good subsequences end in that exact value, plus the sum of all elements across those subsequences.
When you see a new number x, every good subsequence currently ending in x-1 or x+1 can be extended by appending x, and x also forms a brand-new good subsequence all by itself.

Add up the sums of all these newly formed subsequences (the lone x, plus every extended one) into the running answer, then fold those new subsequences into the counts and sums tracked for value x -- previous entries for x from earlier positions stay valid and separate.

edge_inputs.description: a short run with a repeated value
three consecutive values with no repeats
all elements at the minimum bound
values with gaps larger than one never chain together
a long alternating run that chains back and forth between two values
descending consecutive values still chain, since the difference is exactly one in absolute value

stress.description: random arrays clustered around a small range of values to encourage lots of chaining
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 5000)))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 40) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
